channel.title: Dan Patrick Show (Filtered)
channel.description: Daily DP Show episodes without C&R or Best Of clips.
channel.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Are Horses Athletes?, Jim Jackson
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes consider whether a horse should be considered an athlete after watching an exciting Kentucky Derby over the weekend. And NBA on Prime analyst Jim Jackson weighs in on Jaylen Brown's comments and previews the NBA Conference Semifinals.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – American Songwriters, Best and Worst
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes discuss a recently released list of the greatest living American songwriters and debate who should have been included. And they discuss the best and worst of the weekend.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Kentucky Derby Still Slaps, Sour Grapes from Jaylen Brown
item.description:
Dan thoroughly enjoyed the Kentucky Derby over the weekend and appreciates the timelessness of the event. And he weighs in on the NBA Playoffs and Jaylen Brown’s contention that the officials have an “agenda” after the Celtics were eliminated by the Philadelphia 76ers.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Met Gala Madness, Reggie Miller
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes find themselves inexplicably talking about the Met Gala and exactly what goes on there. Plus, Hall of Famer and friend of the show, Reggie Miller stops by to break down the NBA Playoffs and chop it up with DP.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – R.I.P. John Sterling, Cherie DeVaux
item.description:
Dan honors Yankees legendary play-by-play broadcaster John Sterling after his passing yesterday. And trainer of Kentucky Derby winner Golden Temp, Cherie DeVaux drops by to talk about her historic weekend at Churchill Downs.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Knicks Want to Believe, Draymond Green Vs. Austin Rivers
item.description:
Dan digs into the NBA Playoffs now that Round 2 is officially underway, and he thinks Knicks fans finally have a real reason to believe they have a shot. And he weighs in on the beef growing between Draymond Green and Austin Rivers.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Dabo Swinney Gets Loose, Dave McMenamin
item.description:
Clemson HC Dabo Swinney complains about the unfair advantage that schools who can affords higher payrolls have but DP has no sympathy. And NBA insider Dave McMenamin joins the show and talks about sitting down with LeBron James for a long-form interview.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Tim Legler, NFL Post-Draft Power Rankings
item.description:
NBA insider Tim Legler drops by to weigh in on Round 2 of the NBA Playoffs. Plus, DP and the Danettes drop their NFL power rankings now that we are well past the draft.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – NBA Playoffs Recap, High Maintenance Celebrities
item.description:
DP breaks down last night’s NBA action including LeBron making excuses and James Harden looking old. And a discussion about some of the strangest demands made by celebrities and superstar athletes breaks out.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 –College Football Playoff Expansion, NFL Embracing Streaming
item.description:
Dan sees the college football playoffs headed to 24 teams, but he’s not sure it’s what he wants despite all the push to make it happen. The NFL wants to split up Monday night double headers with streaming…but is it worth it?

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Saving Horse Racing’s Triple Crown, Vincent Goodwill
item.description:
The Kentucky Derby winner won’t run in the Preakness for the Triple Crown and Dan says there probably needs to be an appearance fee offered just to keep the interest up. NBA insider Vincent Goodwill joins Dan to discuss the NBA playoffs, how we don’t respect Charles Barkley’s career as much as we should, and the prospects of the Thunder repeating.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Playoffs Blowouts, Draymond Needs a Sense of Humor
item.description:
The San Antonio Spurs embarrassed the Minnesota Timberwolves to even up their 2nd round NBA Playoffs series at a game each so Dan and the Danettes discuss the anatomy of a blowout. Draymond Green and Charles Barkley spar on television, and Dan thinks if Draymond wants to become a big media figure he needs to relax and not take everything so personally.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Reputations Made in the NBA Playoffs
item.description:
Dan talks about one of his favorite NBA playoff games he can remember and considers which current players are watching their reputations change, for good or bad, as their performances take center stage.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – SGA: Foul Merchant, Charles Barkley
item.description:
Callers weigh in on flopping in the NBA after the incidents in the Lakers/Thunder from Wednesday night with Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Chet Holmgren fishing for fouls. Inside the NBA host and NBA legend Charles Barkley drops by to address the very public beef he has going on with Warriors star Draymond Green.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – James Harden and LeBron James Both Down for Different Reasons, NCAA Tournament expands
item.description:
Cade Cunningham is must-see TV, but in this series between the Cavaliers and Pistons Dan can readily see the shortcomings of James Harden. The Lakers are mad they’re not getting officiating calls against the Thunder as LeBron gets clobbered. College basketball is expanding the NCAA Men’s Basketball Tournament to 76 teams as sports continue to chase the live TV windows.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Eastern Conference Woes, Jamal Crawford
item.description:
Dan talks about the Indiana Pacers adventure in the NBA Draft Lottery and the inconsistent Eastern Conference of the NBA. And former NBA standout and current analyst, Jamal Crawford praises Anthony Edwards' competitiveness and would be on himself over any other current NBA analyst working today on a straight up, 1-on-1 competition on the court. 

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – LeBron’s Future Decoded, Best and Worst of the Weekend
item.description:
DP and the Danettes consider whether there is any possibility of LeBron James retiring and try to imagine what his exit from the NBA will look like.  And they go around the room to discuss the best and worst of an eventful weekend.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Wemby Needs and Enforcer, NBA Draft Lottery
item.description:
San Antonio Spurs superstar Victor Wembanyama was ejected for a vicious elbow yesterday in their loss to the Timberwolves and Dan thinks Wemby needs somebody to throw those elbows for him. And DP wonders if we have seen the last of tanking in the NBA after the NBA Draft Lottery took place over the weekend.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Ryan McGee, Reggie Miller
item.description:
College football insider Ryan McGee sees Lane Kiffin's comments as unnecessary and says this is all part of the Lane Kiffin experience. Hall of Famer Reggie Miller backs the NBA's decision not to suspend Wemby and would take Jalen Brunson over Donovan Mitchell. 

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Birthday Prep, Nick Wright
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes talk about what, if anything, they will be doing to celebrate DP’s birthday. And Nick Wright drops by to chop it up with DP and weigh in on LeBron’s future now that the Lakers have been eliminated from the NBA Playoffs.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Is OKC in the Conversation?, The Future of LeBron and the Lakers
item.description:
The Oklahoma City Thunder haven’t lost a game in these NBA Playoffs as they enter the Western Conference Finals and DP considers what it will take to put them in the conversation of greatest teams in NBA history. And he dives into speculation about the future of LeBron James and the Los Angeles Lakers and whether King James will remain in LA.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Paul Skenes Is Dominating, Salary Caps
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes talk a little hardball as Pirates’ phenom Paul Skenes continues to dominate, especially in the month of May. And they discuss the need to manage salary caps in professional sports, and which franchises are making it work.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Martin Luther Kiffin, James Worthy
item.description:
DP rips into LUS HC Lane Kiffin for taking shots at Ole Miss as well as his tone-deaf apology. And Hall of Fame NBA legend, James Worthy drops by to talk some hoops and weigh in on LeBron’s possible future with (or without) the Lakers.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Spurs’ Future Is Bright, NFL International Games
item.description:
DP and the Danettes break down the Spurs’ win last night, Wemby’s dominance, and the bright future that appears to await the Spurs. And more NFL schedule “leaks” as the international game locations are revealed.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 3 – Stan Van Gundy, Don’t Be Late!
item.description:
Former NBA coach and current commentator Stan Van Gundy discusses the issues Pistons coach JB Bickerstaff is having with the officials, what the Lakers should do with LeBron James and talks about who he thinks is the best player in the NBA right now. A golfer doesn’t show up on time to the PGA Championship and the Danettes try to work through that scenario.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – Running Back Renaissance, Shohei Is Dealing
item.description:
Dan talks about the running back position in the NFL making a comeback after years of being devalued. And he chimes in on Shohei Ohtani’s early dominance on the mound this season despite his hitting struggles.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Pistons Don’t Get the Call, Lakers and Steelers Should Move on
item.description:
DP thinks it was an obvious tripping foul in the waning moments of last night’s NBA Playoff game between the Cavaliers and Pistons and he’s tired of the inconsistent officiating when games are on the line. And he believes the Lakers and Steelers would be making mistakes if they hold on to their aging superstars (LeBron James and Aaron Rodgers) instead of building for the future.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 2 – NFL Schedule Breakdown, Albert Breer
item.description:
Dan and the Danettes dive deep into the NFL schedule and some of the most intriguing matchups. And NFL insider Albert Breer joins the show to discuss the NFL schedule, the ongoing Mike Vrabel saga, and more storylines as the NFL once again seizes headlines.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick

item.title: Hour 1 – Happy Birthday DP!, NFL Opening Game Drama
item.description:
DP is celebrating his 70th birthday and the Danettes scramble to present appropriate gifts. The NFL schedule was released and although the opening game is a Super Bowl rematch between the Seahawks and Patriots, all that anybody is interested in is talking about Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: Dan Patrick
